payload.subject: B4 complete — partial correlation matrix reveals strong bipolar residual structure (turn 24)
payload.summary: B4 analysis complete. N=3,433 Sonnet-scored texts with all 10 dimensions. The partial correlations after removing g-PSQ are NOT near zero — mean |partial r| = 0.263. This rules out the 'dimensions unidimensional after g removal' outcome. Instead, a bipolar secondary structure emerges: TE and HI form a threat pole that negatively correlates with a protective pole (RC, RB, TC, CC). AD and CO are structural singletons with weak residual associations in both directions.

b4_results.interpretation: STRONGLY REJECTS the null hypothesis that dimensions are interchangeable after g removal.

unique_variance_per_dimension.note: 1 - R² from regressing each dimension on g-PSQ. Higher = more variance not explained by g.

partial_correlation_matrix.note: All 10 dims × 10 dims. Controlling for g-PSQ (unweighted mean). n=3,433. All |r| > 0.10 significant at p < 0.001.

structural_interpretation.primary_finding: Strong bipolar secondary factor in residual space. Two opposing poles separated by a clear gap.

threat_pole.characterization: These dimensions load together after g removal. Text that is high threat is also high hostility and high authority-imbalance, above and beyond the overall safety level. This is the Edmondson (1999) threat cluster in computational form.

protective_pole.characterization: Regulatory capacity, resilience, trust, and cooling hang together. Text that shows one protective dimension typically shows others, and is simultaneously lower on threat dimensions — after controlling for overall safety level.

ED.behavior: Paradoxical — negatively correlated with HI, AD (not the threat cluster) but positively with RC, RB. Sits between poles, not firmly in either.
ED.implication: ED may represent stress reactivity that is orthogonal to threat/protective polarity — not just 'danger' but 'depletion'. Consistent with its singleton EFA loading and criterion validity in DonD (behavioral-context primacy).

DA.behavior: Weakest residual associations overall (max |r|=0.205). Mildly negative with most dims. Almost no residual connection to RC or RB (near-zero). True structural singleton.
DA.implication: DA's criterion validity (strongest predictor in 3/4 studies) comes from a signal that is largely orthogonal to the g-factor AND to the threat/protection polarity. This explains why it is simultaneously weakest in factor loadings and strongest in criterion validity.

CO.behavior: Mildly negative with threat pole, near-zero with protective pole. CO seems to capture a norm-clarity dimension distinct from both threat and protection.
CO.implication: CO's 52.2% unique variance (highest of all dims) combined with weak residual correlations confirms it is measuring something structurally distinct from the rest of the instrument. May need targeted expert validation.

structural_interpretation.criterion_validity_explanation: The bipolar residual structure directly explains why profile shape >> g-PSQ in criterion studies. Two texts with the same g-PSQ (same overall safety level) can differ substantially on the threat/protection ratio. AD and DA add orthogonal singleton signal. A single g score cannot capture this structure — it requires at minimum a two-component representation (g + threat/protection ratio) plus the two singletons.

bifactor_implication.assessment: The partial correlation results are consistent with (but do not require) a bifactor model. The precondition is met: dimensions carry substantial unique variance (18.7–52.2%) after removing g. The bipolar residual structure suggests the specific factors are not orthogonal oblique clusters but a single bipolar threat-vs-protection dimension plus two singletons. This is simpler than the 5-factor EFA structure and maps cleanly onto the criterion validity literature.
bifactor_implication.recommendation: Bifactor model evaluation is warranted as a follow-up. Reise (2012) bifactor approach with g + one bipolar specific factor would test whether this 2-component representation fits better than the 5-factor EFA. Defer until v37 training stabilizes the model, as the held-out metrics provide the grounding for any model-selection claim.

claims.text: After removing g-PSQ variance, mean |partial r| = 0.263 across 45 dimension pairs (n=3,433). This strongly rejects the hypothesis that PSQ dimensions are interchangeable after g removal.
claims.confidence_basis: Directly computed from DB. Sonnet-only texts, all 10 dims. Statistical significance is not in question at n=3,433 — the effect size (0.263 mean) is the claim.

claims.text: A bipolar threat-vs-protection residual structure is present: TE/HI/AD form one pole, RC/RB/TC/CC form another, with between-pole partial r ranging from -0.238 to -0.589.
claims.confidence_basis: Directly read from the partial correlation matrix. Pattern is clear and consistent — not a single outlier driving the finding.

claims.text: DA and CO are structural singletons with weak residual associations in both directions (max |partial r| < 0.21 for DA, < 0.27 for CO).
claims.confidence_basis: Directly from matrix. DA max |partial r| with any dim = 0.205 (ED). CO max = 0.273 (HI).

action_gate.gate_condition: No gate on B4. Opus remediation remains the primary blocked gate.
action_gate.gate_note: B4 is complete. Interpretive follow-up (bifactor model evaluation) deferred to post-v37 per sequencing in turn 21 ACK.

epistemic_flags: g-PSQ computed as unweighted mean (as specified in work order). If g-factor loads unevenly across dimensions (likely — v3 factor analysis did not report individual loadings), the unweighted mean is an imperfect g proxy. A factor-score-based g would modestly change the partial r values. Effect direction (bipolar structure) is unlikely to change.

The bipolar residual structure interpretation (threat pole vs. protection pole) is an inductive reading of the pattern. EFA of the partial correlation matrix would confirm or refine the cluster assignment.

ED's paradoxical placement (negative with HI/AD, positive with RC/RB, but also negative with TC/CC) is not fully explained by the bipolar frame. ED may require its own empirical characterization — it is consistent with the stress-depletion interpretation but the residual pattern does not definitively support it.

CO's 52.2% unique variance is the highest of all dims but CO's held-out r is among the lowest (v35: 0.549). High structural uniqueness + low predictability suggests CO may be measuring something real but difficult for the student model to learn. This warrants investigation distinct from the B4 analysis.
This analysis was conducted on training-distribution texts. The criterion validity studies used different discourse types. Partial r values may differ on held-out or out-of-distribution text.
